evals.name: Build a two-compartment PK model
evals.description: Tests multi-compartment model construction including inter-compartment reactions, which require correct species qualification to avoid ambiguous species errors.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Build a two-compartment PK model with a Central compartment (Drug, initial amount 100) and a Peripheral compartment (DrugP, initial amount 0). Add elimination from Central (ke=0.1, MassAction), distribution from Central to Peripheral (k12=0.3, MassAction), and redistribution from Peripheral to Central (k21=0.15, MassAction). Also add an absorption reaction (null -> Drug) with rate ka*100*exp(-ka*time) where ka=0.5. Verify by simulating 24 hours and showing Drug values at t=0, 6, 12, and 24.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches ambiguous species failures and incorrect API usage
Model has the correct structure: 2 compartments, 2 species, 4 reactions
assertions.expected: 2 compartments, 2 species, 4 reactions

assertions.description: Simulation produces a valid PK profile — Drug decreases over time
assertions.expected: Drug values at t=0, 6, 12, 24 displayed; Drug at t=24 < Drug at t=0

assertions.description: Agent does not use deprecated InitialAmount property (Rule 6)
assertions.expected: InitialAmount

assertions.description: Agent does not use deprecated ConstantAmount property (Rule 6)
assertions.expected: ConstantAmount

evals.name: Add doses, observables, and variants to existing model
evals.description: Tests dose creation (schedule vs repeat types), observable expressions, and variant construction — areas where incorrect type selection or expression syntax causes errors.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/OneCompPK.sbproj. Add: (1) a single 500mg bolus dose at time 0, (2) a repeat dose of 200mg every 12 hours for 5 total doses, (3) an observable called HalfLife that computes 0.693 divided by ke, and (4) a variant called HighClearance that sets ke to 0.5. Confirm everything was created correctly and simulate with the repeat dose to show it works.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches wrong dose types, observable expression syntax errors, and variant format issues
Model has correct component counts after additions
assertions.expected: 2 new doses, observable, and variant confirmed on the model

assertions.description: Simulation with repeat dose runs and produces valid output
assertions.expected: Simulation completes with dose-driven Drug profile

evals.name: Modify and remove model components
evals.description: Tests component removal and modification — specifically reaction lookup and dose removal, which use different patterns than other SimBiology objects.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/OneCompPK.sbproj. Make these changes: (1) Remove the elimination reaction. (2) Remove all doses. (3) Add a slower metabolism pathway (Drug -> null) with rate kmet=0.05 using MassAction. Show me the updated model structure.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches incorrect reaction lookup and dose removal patterns
Model structure is correct after modifications: metabolism reaction present, elimination gone, no doses
assertions.expected: kmet parameter, metabolism reaction present, no elimination, 0 doses

evals.name: Diagram a two-compartment model with varied species names
evals.description: Tests diagram layout with species of different name lengths, requiring content-aware sizing and compartment containment.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/TwoCompPK_Extended.mat and create a diagram for it.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error
Diagram has no overlap violations — species with long names (DrugAlbuminComplex) do not overlap other blocks
assertions.expected: checkDiagramLayout reports 0 overlap violations, or no overlapping blocks reported

assertions.description: Diagram passes layout validation with no violations
assertions.expected: checkDiagramLayout reports 0 total violations (nTotal = 0 or 'Remaining violations: 0')

assertions.description: All species are contained within their parent compartment boundaries
assertions.expected: 0 containment violations reported by checkDiagramLayout

evals.name: Diagram a 4-compartment PKPD model
evals.description: Tests complex diagram layout with multiple connected compartments, requiring logical spatial organization and clean reaction routing.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Load the model from evals/files/PKPD_4Comp.mat and create a diagram for it. Central is the main hub compartment connecting to the others — position it accordingly.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error
Central compartment is positioned reflecting its hub role — adjacent to other compartments
assertions.expected: Central positioned centrally or adjacently to connected compartments

assertions.description: Diagram passes layout validation with no violations
assertions.expected: checkDiagramLayout reports 0 total violations (nTotal = 0 or 'Remaining violations: 0')

evals.name: Builder lifecycle and no-regression checks
evals.description: Tests that the skill handles the Builder app correctly: opens safely when asked, does not open when not asked, and cleans up properly before session reset.
evals.prompt:
Setup: close any open SimBiology apps and run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: close apps and run `sbioreset` when done.

Do the following three tasks in order:

1. Build a one-compartment model (Drug=100, ke=0.1, elimination MassAction).

2. Now open the model in the Model Builder so I can see the diagram.

3. I'm done — clean up the session so I can start fresh next time.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error
Step 1 does not open the Builder — model construction is programmatic
assertions.expected: No simBiologyModelBuilder or openLiveBuilder call during model construction phase

assertions.description: Step 2 opens the Builder successfully without error
assertions.expected: Builder opens and displays the model diagram without throwing an error

assertions.description: Step 3 closes apps before resetting the model root — sbioreset does not close apps automatically
assertions.expected: App close operations precede sbioreset

assertions.description: Session is cleaned up — apps closed and sbioreset called
assertions.expected: Both app closure and sbioreset performed during cleanup

evals.name: Build receptor-ligand model with correct diagram arrows
evals.description: Tests whether the agent writes reactions in the correct direction so the diagram renders arrows on products and plain lines on reactants. Writing a binding reaction backwards produces incorrect arrows.
evals.prompt:
Setup: close any open SimBiology apps and run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: close apps and run `sbioreset` when done.

Create a model of receptor-ligand kinetics: ligand L (initial 5 nM) binds receptor R (initial 10 nM) to form complex C (initial 0). The forward binding rate is kf=0.05 and dissociation rate is kr=0.1. Show me the model in the Builder app. The diagram should show L and R connected to the reaction with plain lines (they're consumed) and C connected with an arrow (it's produced).

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error and diagram opens in Builder
Reaction is structured correctly: L and R are reactants, C is product
assertions.expected: Reaction string shows L + R on left side of arrow, C on right side

evals.name: Build standard 1-compartment PK model with clearance parameterization
evals.description: Tests whether the agent produces a PK model with the standard clearance/volume parameterization (ke = CL/V) rather than just a bare ke constant — matching what the PK library produces.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Create a one-compartment PK model with bolus IV dosing and linear clearance elimination. Use the standard pharmacokinetic parameterization where clearance and volume are the primary parameters. Set CL=5 L/hr and V=50 L. Simulate a 500mg IV bolus for 24 hours and show me the concentration-time profile.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error
Model has clearance as a parameter with ke derived from CL/V
assertions.expected: Clearance parameter present; ke = CL/V relationship established (via rule or direct calculation)

assertions.description: Simulation produces correct initial concentration and exponential decay
assertions.expected: C(0) = 500/50 = 10 mg/L; C(24) approximately 10*exp(-0.1*24) = 0.9 mg/L

evals.name: Build model with nested compartments that share biological names
evals.description: Tests that the agent handles nested compartments where the biological description tempts reuse of compartment names across levels. The key trap: a Tissue contains an 'Intracellular' space and an 'Extracellular' space, and a second Tissue also has its own 'Intracellular' and 'Extracellular' — but SimBiology requires globally unique compartment names.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Build a two-tissue distribution model. The body has two tissues: Muscle and Liver. Each tissue has an intracellular space and an extracellular space. The drug distributes between the intracellular and extracellular spaces within each tissue, and between the extracellular spaces and the blood plasma.

Specifically:
- Plasma compartment: Drug (initial 100)
- Muscle tissue: has an extracellular space (Drug initial 0) and an intracellular space (Drug initial 0)
- Liver tissue: has an extracellular space (Drug initial 0) and an intracellular space (Drug initial 0)

Reactions (all MassAction):
1. Plasma Drug <-> Muscle extracellular Drug (k_in=0.3, k_out=0.2)
2. Plasma Drug <-> Liver extracellular Drug (k_in=0.5, k_out=0.4)
3. Muscle extracellular Drug <-> Muscle intracellular Drug (k_perm_m=0.1, k_perm_m_out=0.05)
4. Liver extracellular Drug <-> Liver intracellular Drug (k_perm_l=0.2, k_perm_l_out=0.1)
5. Liver intracellular Drug -> null (metabolism, k_met=0.08)

Nest the tissue compartments appropriately. Simulate 24 hours and show Drug in all 5 compartments at t=0, 6, 12, 24.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches duplicate compartment names (e.g., two compartments both named 'Intracellular') and invalid multi-level qualification (e.g., 'Muscle.Intracellular.Drug')
All 5 compartments have globally unique names — no duplicates
assertions.expected: 5 compartments with distinct names (e.g., Plasma, Muscle_EC, Muscle_IC, Liver_EC, Liver_IC or similar unique naming scheme)

assertions.description: Reactions use single-level qualification only — no multi-dot paths
assertions.expected: All reaction strings use 'CompartmentName.Species' format with a single dot, never 'Parent.Child.Species'

assertions.description: Simulation produces valid dynamics — plasma Drug decreases as tissues absorb
assertions.expected: Plasma Drug decreases from 100; tissue compartments accumulate drug; liver intracellular decreases due to metabolism

evals.name: Build model with rules and events requiring Constant=false
evals.description: Tests whether the agent correctly sets Constant=false on parameters before using them as rule LHS targets or event action targets — a common source of runtime errors that the skill explicitly teaches.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Build a receptor-occupancy model: a single compartment with species Target (initial 100), Drug (initial 50), and Complex (initial 0). Add a binding reaction Drug + Target -> Complex (kon=0.01, MassAction) and dissociation Complex -> Drug + Target (koff=0.1, MassAction).

Then add:
1. A computed parameter called RO (receptor occupancy) defined by a repeated-assignment rule: RO = Complex / (Target + Complex)
2. An event: when RO exceeds 0.8, halve the kon rate constant (kon = kon * 0.5)

Simulate for 50 time units and show me RO over time.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — catches 'Cannot set the value of a constant' errors from missing Constant=false
No 'Cannot set the value of a constant' error — proves Constant=false was set on rule LHS parameter
assertions.expected: Cannot set the value of a constant

assertions.description: No 'Unable to evaluate' error — proves Constant=false was set on event target parameter
assertions.expected: Unable to evaluate

assertions.description: Simulation shows RO increasing from 0 as Complex forms
assertions.expected: RO starts at 0 (no complex yet) and increases over time as binding occurs; values displayed

evals.name: Save model to file and reload it
evals.description: Tests that the agent uses the modern save pattern (save to .mat) rather than the deprecated sbiosaveproject. The skill explicitly teaches this pattern.
evals.prompt:
Setup: run `sbioreset` before starting. Teardown: run `sbioreset` when done.

Build a simple one-compartment model: Drug (initial 100), ke=0.1, elimination via MassAction. Then save it to a file so I can reload it in a future session. After saving, clear the workspace and reload the model from the file to prove the round-trip works. Show me the model structure after reloading.

assertions.description: All generated code runs without error — save and reload complete successfully
Agent does not use deprecated sbiosaveproject
assertions.expected: sbiosaveproject(

assertions.description: Model is saved using save() to a .mat file
assertions.expected: save() function used with .mat extension; FAIL if sbiosaveproject or sbioexport used instead

assertions.description: Reloaded model has the same structure as the original
assertions.expected: After load(), model has 1 compartment, 1 species (Drug), 1 parameter (ke), 1 reaction
